title: Minimum Number of Plumbing Fixtures
text: Plumbing fixtures shall be provided for the type of occupancy and in the minimum number shown in Table 403.1: business water closets = 1 per 25 (first 50) + 1 per 50 over 50, with separate facilities for each sex; assembly = 1 per 75 (male) / 1 per 65 (female); residential = 1 water closet, 1 lavatory, 1 bath or shower, 1 kitchen sink per dwelling unit; educational classroom = 1 WC per 50; mercantile = 1 WC per 500.

title: Water Closet Compartment Spacing
text: The water closet stool in a private toilet room shall have a minimum clearance of 15 inches (381 mm) from its center to any side wall, partition, vanity, or other obstruction, or not less than 30 inches (762 mm) center to center between adjacent fixtures. There shall be at least a 21-inch (533 mm) clearance in front of the water closet.

title: Water Service Pipe Sizing
text: Water service pipe shall be a minimum of 3/4 inch (19.1 mm) nominal size and shall be sized to deliver the required water demand for the building or premises to which it is connected. Sizing per Tables E103.3.2 and E103.3.3 based on water supply fixture units and developed length, accounting for pressure available.

title: Backflow Prevention
text: Connections of pipes and fixtures to the potable water supply shall be made in a manner that will prevent backflow of water or other substances into the supply. Air gap, atmospheric vacuum breaker, pressure vacuum breaker, double-check valve assembly, or reduced pressure principle backflow preventer required based on hazard level (high vs low) per Section 608.13.

title: Drain Waste and Vent Sizing
text: The minimum size of horizontal drain piping serving fixtures and of vertical vents shall be determined based on drainage fixture unit (DFU) values from Table 709.1. Every fixture trap shall be vented. Vents shall be sized so the developed length and DFU load do not exceed the values in Table 906.1. Minimum vent diameter 1-1/4 inch (32 mm).

title: Building Sewer Slope
text: Horizontal drainage pipes 3 inches (76 mm) and smaller shall have a minimum slope of 1/4 unit vertical in 12 units horizontal (2-percent slope). Pipes 4 inches (102 mm) through 6 inches (152 mm) shall have a minimum slope of 1/8 unit vertical in 12 units horizontal (1-percent slope). Pipes 8 inches (203 mm) and larger may have 1/16-inch per foot slope where approved.

title: Ventilation Rates
text: The minimum ventilation rate of required outdoor airflow shall be determined in accordance with Table 403.3.1.1 by combined Rp (per-person rate) and Ra (per-area rate). Common values: office space Rp = 5 cfm/person, Ra = 0.06 cfm/sf at default 5 occupants per 1000 sf; classroom 10 cfm/person + 0.12; conference 5 + 0.06; retail 7.5 + 0.12; restaurant dining 7.5 + 0.18.

title: Bathroom and Kitchen Exhaust
text: Exhaust airflow rate per Table 403.3.1.1: bathroom or toilet room = 50 cfm intermittent or 20 cfm continuous; kitchen exhaust hood for residential = 100 cfm intermittent or 25 cfm continuous when not located over the cooktop, or as recommended by manufacturer when over cooktop. Commercial kitchens require Type I or Type II hoods per Section 507.

title: Condensate Disposal
text: Condensate from cooling coils and evaporators shall be conveyed from the drain pan outlet to an approved place of disposal. Condensate shall not discharge into a street, alley, or other areas where it would cause a nuisance. Auxiliary drain pan or float switch required where condensate damage could result; pan must be at least 3 inches larger than the unit and connected to a separate drain or have float switch shut off unit.

title: Duct Insulation
text: Supply and return air ducts and plenums shall be insulated as specified in Table 603.6 based on location and climate zone. Ducts in unconditioned spaces: minimum R-6 in climate zones 1-2, R-8 in zones 3-8. Ducts outside the building: R-8 minimum. Ducts and plenums located within conditioned spaces are not required to be insulated.

title: Combustion Air
text: Combustion air for fuel-fired appliances shall be provided in accordance with the International Fuel Gas Code (IFGC). Indoor combustion air opening method: 50 cubic feet of room volume per 1000 BTU/hr of total input rating. Outdoor combustion air openings: 1 sq inch per 4000 BTU/hr for combined high and low openings, or 1 sq inch per 3000 BTU/hr for a single opening.
